comment_text.sn22.76:2.4: The nine “abodes of sentient beings” (sattāvāsā) are listed at (AN 9.24. The “pinnacle of existence” (bhavagga), also mentioned in passing at AN 4.75:1.3, is the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception (Vb 18:209.3, Kv 3.12:22.4).
comment_text.sn22.76:8.1: The “ten qualities of an adept” are the eight path factors plus right knowledge and right freedom (AN 10.112).
comment_text.sn22.76:10.1: Vikampati is also used in the context of the threefold conceit at SN 1.20:23.3 = Snp 4.9:8.3 and in the verses of Mahākassapa at Thag 18.1:26.4.

root_text.sn22.76:1.5: yadanattā taṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn22.76:1.12: yadanattā taṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn22.76:2.1: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpasmimpi nibbindati, vedanāyapi … saññāyapi … saṅkhāresupi … viññāṇasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.sn22.76:2.2: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati. Vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn22.76:2.3: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn22.76:2.4: Yāvatā, bhikkhave, sattāvāsā, yāvatā bhavaggaṁ, ete aggā, ete seṭṭhā lokasmiṁ yadidaṁ arahanto”ti.
root_text.sn22.76:3.2: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato athāparaṁ etadavoca satthā:

translation_text.sn22.76:1.5: And what’s not-self should be truly seen with right understanding like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn22.76:1.12: And what’s not-self should be truly seen with right understanding like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn22.76:2.1: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.
translation_text.sn22.76:2.2: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn22.76:2.3: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn22.76:2.4: As far as there are abodes of sentient beings, even up until the pinnacle of existence, the perfected ones are the foremost and the best.”
translation_text.sn22.76:3.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn22.76:3.2: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn22.76:4.1: “Oh! How happy are the perfected ones!
translation_text.sn22.76:4.2: Craving is not found in them,
translation_text.sn22.76:4.3: the conceit ‘I am’ is cut off,
translation_text.sn22.76:4.4: and the net of delusion is shattered.
translation_text.sn22.76:5.3: nothing in the world clings to them,
translation_text.sn22.76:6.2: their domain is the seven good qualities.
translation_text.sn22.76:7.2: and trained in the three trainings,
translation_text.sn22.76:7.4: with fear and dread given up.
translation_text.sn22.76:8.2: those giants are immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.sn22.76:8.3: They are the best in the world,
translation_text.sn22.76:8.4: craving is not found in them.
translation_text.sn22.76:9.2: ‘This bag of bones is my last.’
translation_text.sn22.76:9.4: in the core of the spiritual path.
translation_text.sn22.76:10.1: They do not waver due to the discriminations,
translation_text.sn22.76:10.3: They’ve reached the level of the tamed,
translation_text.sn22.76:10.4: in the world, they’re the winners.
translation_text.sn22.76:11.2: relishing is not found in them.
translation_text.sn22.76:11.4: ‘The awakened are supreme in the world!’”
